q: The electrons are filled in the lower energy atomic orbitals after filling higher energy ones.
Silicon dioxide conducts electricity because it contains free electrons.
Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to attract electrons.
In solid ionic compounds, their ions are held in variable positions and cannot move.
London dispersion forces can be formed between non-polar molecules.
Metals conduct electricity when solid because positive ions are free to move.
The single bonds are the longest and stronger than double and triple bonds.
A coordinate bond is a special type of covalent bond.
Hydrogen bonding can occur in liquid water.
An ionic bond involves electron sharing between two atoms.
According to Pauli's exclusion principle, the two electrons in the same orbital will have parallel spins.
The d-subshell has five dumbbell-shaped orbitals.
The two electrons with the same spin can occupy the same orbital.
Hund's rule states that an electron occupies the lowest energy orbital first.
The shape of BF₃ is a trigonal planar geometry, so it is considered nonpolar.
The higher the value of the electronegativity, the greater is the atom's attraction to electrons.
Some compounds have both ionic and covalent character.
The s-subshell has only one spherical-shaped orbital.
In a covalent bond there are complete transfer of electrons between two atoms.
VSEPR theory is a theory to predict the three-dimensional shapes of molecules.
All the inorganic and organic materials are not created with chemical bonds.
Graphite contains unbonded electrons which move through the structure.
The amount of energy required to add an electron from a gaseous atom.
According to Pauli's exclusion principle, no more two electrons in an atom can have same orbital.
Metallic bond is a strong force of attraction between the metal ions and the electrons.
The covalent bonds can be polar or non-polar.
Ion-dipole interactions are formed between a metal and a non-metal.
Hydrogen bonding can occur in intermolecular forces only.
Silicon dioxide is a giant covalent molecule with four oxygen atoms bonded to each silicon atom.
Hydrogen bonds involve hydrogen bonded to carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, or fluorine.
H₂O molecule is an example of the formation of dative bond.
The structure of silicon dioxide is similar to graphite.
Metals conduct electricity when molten because negative ions are free to move.
The elements having high electron affinity easily lose electrons.
Silicon(IV) oxide, SiO₂ (silica or quartz) is a giant covalent molecule.
A hydrogen bond is a special type of dipole-dipole interaction.
Everything around us is made up of elements, molecules, or compounds.
CO₂ molecule has no dipole moment since C-O bonds are nonpolar.
A metal loses electrons to form positively charged ions (cations).
In a graphite, each carbon atom is joined to four other carbon atoms by covalent bonds.
The strength of metallic bond is higher than that of an ionic bond.
The elements having high ionisation energy results in the formation of the cations.
An ionic bond is the attractive force between a positive ion and a negative ion.
Atoms will lose, gain, or share electrons to obtain an octet.
Carbon is in Group VI and has 6 valence electrons.
According to Pauli's principle, all the electrons in singly occupied orbitals have the same spin.
The water molecule is a non-polar molecule.
HCN is a linear molecule, therefore it is a nonpolar molecule.
In ionic compounds, the cations and anions are held together by strong electrostatic attraction.
The covalent bond is the electrostatic attraction between the nuclei of the atoms and the transferred electrons.
CO molecule has a triple bond that consists of two covalent bonds and one coordinate bond.
Metals consist of giant structures of atoms.
